violations.text: Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of PIC, Chicken broth was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Food items were moved into smaller hotel pans to cool properly. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2026

Cooked Lobster Meat was observed thawing in the center island cold hold unit with intact reduced oxygen packaging (ROP). Seafood items packaged in ROP packaging shall be completely removed from its original packaging prior to thawing to allow the reintroduction of oxygen into the food item. Food item was observed at 30 degrees Fahrenheit and was removed from its original packaging during the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2026

The floors in the basement preparation area are constructed of uncoated cement, which is not durable, easily cleanable, or non-absorbent. The materials for indoor floors must be durable, easily cleanable and nonabsorbent for areas subject to moisture such as food preparation areas, walk-in refrigerators, ware-washing areas, and toilet rooms. New Violation. Correct By: 02/02/2026

The hand sinks in the kitchen and the basement preparation area were observed leaking and in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/16/2025

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the dishwashing area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 12/02/2025
